programme.desc: When an 80-year-old woman is found barely conscious next to the body of her murdered son in an abandoned van, the FBI was brought in and linked the crime to a teenager and middle-aged man.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: After a parole officer was found shot dead in his car, the FBI, DEA and NYPD struggled to contain the mounting violence from drug gangs that continued to escalate. Authorities sought to put two ruthless drug kingpins behind bars for the crime.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A Milwaukee teen was kidnapped by four gunmen who demanded $30,000 from his family for his safe return.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 2000, the FBI was overwhelmed with investigations in Seattle which had the third-highest number of bank robberies in the nation. Agents focused on a series of heists in which two heavily armed men seemed to know where the banks stored their money.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In July 1996, two men entered a pawnshop in Birmingham, Alabama and callously murdered the employees.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A convicted killer serving life for manslaughter is found to be guilty of a second murder 32 years later.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Charles seemed like a helpful nurse, moving easily between hospitals. However, behind the façade, he had been secretly killed patients for years, avoiding suspicion by constantly changing jobs until investigators uncovered his deadly pattern.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A fugitive from the United States tackles the British Police and goes on the run.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the serial killer who brutally snatched away the lives of eight innocent women during a decade-long killing spree in Louisiana.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The chilling story of the serial killer who worked as a nurse in Texas, where she killed many children and infants by injecting them with drugs.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The chilling story of Mark Martin, who murdered three homeless women in his quest to become Nottingham's first serial killer.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In 2022, Danville Neil was finally jailed for the brutal 1993 murders of a WWII veteran and his sister.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Police in Aurora, Colorado found the body of a young man in in his own home with a bullet wound to the head.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Gloria, a devoted mother, tried to stop her son David's forbidden relationship. Her attempt to protect her family ended in tragedy as David's hidden violent streak erupted with devastating consequences.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of 27-year-old Pawel Relowicz, who raped and killed 21-year-old Hull student Libby Squire in a case that made headlines around the UK.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The horrifying story of a computer engineer who lured a 14-year-old school boy to his flat and killed him.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the cold-blooded serial killer who took the lives of eleven people during a terrifying decade-long murder campaign.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the serial killer who brutally snatched away the lives of eight innocent women during a decade-long killing spree in Louisiana.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Dozens of Atlanta youths disappeared and were later found murdered, terrorizing the community. Wayne became the prime suspect, denying involvement even as forensic evidence mounted.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: When Mary Gough was found dead at the bottom of her stairs in her family home, her husband Colin Whelan appeared shocked. But a postmortem revealed her injuries weren't consistent with a fall. Whelan had murdered her for an insurance payout.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In October 2004, Rachel O'Reilly left her home to drop her children off at school. Less than 20 minutes after returning home she was murdered. Her husband, Joe O'Reilly, staged the crime scene to make it look like a burglary gone wrong.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Mary's murder haunted Glasgow for 36 years with no witnesses or leads until advanced forensic techniques finally revealed Graham as the killer, ending decades of uncertainty for her family and investigators.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Jerome Brudos was a serial killer and necrophile who murdered four women in Oregon during the 1960s. He was known as the 'The Lust Killer' and 'The Shoe Fetish Slayer.'

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Who was the sexual offender who murdered two boys in Nebraska? The key to stopping him would be the unique composition of the "junk rope" he used to tie his victims.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Bruce Miller was shot to death in the office of a junkyard business. When a computer forensics expert examined the computers owned by the victim's wife, he found all the evidence needed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a wealthy real estate tycoon goes missing, it appears to be foul play. In his will, he leaves instructions in case he should die under violent circumstances - which were carried out after a hiker comes across his bullet-ridden skull.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On September 17, 1984, in a suburb of Tucson, Arizona, eight-year-old Vicki Hoskins left home on her pink bicycle to mail a letter for her mother. She never returned, but her slightly damaged bicycle was found nearby.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman's death in Pennsylvania triggers a homicide investigation into another woman's death in North Carolina.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Dianna Green was brutally attacked in her own home and her unborn child was killed. After coming out of a coma and regaining her memory, she is able to identify her husband as the perpetrator.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An Alaskan police officer finds a woman's body while on routine patrol of a public park. During the autopsy, the medical examiner preserves the portion of her rib cage which sustained the fatal wound.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: California police had several suspects in the robbery and brutal murder of an elderly couple. Ten years later, a determined forensic scientist used material from a dentist's office to uncover ballistic evidence that had been overlooked.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1991, a high school girl vanished after attending a party. The prime suspect had two alibi witnesses for the night she disappeared. A solid alibi can often overcome circumstantial evidence, but forensic evidence is another matter.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: For 15 months, a serial killer was strangling prostitutes in Florida then taunting police by leaving the bodies in plain sight. The only clues were a tire impression and some threads.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man tells police he shot an intruder who killed his wife. Forensic evidence disproves that story later on.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A healthy young man dies mysteriously in his sleep, and toxicology tests reveal a lethal dose of lidocaine in his blood.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a policeman's house goes up in flames, experts suspect arson. The officer whose home was torched arrests a low-level drug dealer, who confesses. But then the suspect vanishes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1996, when a fundamentalist group starts attacking and robbing banks in Spokane, Washington, authorities know immediately that they are dealing with experienced criminals.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police suspect Dr. John Boyle in the disappearance of his wife when he buys a new house with a woman pretending to be his spouse. The police later find a body in the basement of his new house.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In the mid-1980s, bodies of nude women were found on a remote hill of California. The police got a break when a known prostitute escaped an attacker and was able to identify him and his automobile.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a serial killer was terrorizing the city of St. Louis, the FBI didn't need lab tests, fingerprints or any of the usual methods. All they needed was a computer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police investigate a discarded sleeping bag containing bloody sneakers and a purse, which eventually leads to the body of a young woman. A mark found on the victim's body enables police to track the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Two people witness a truck driving over an unconscious man and flee into a nearby bar, but when the police turn up, the truck, the man and the witnesses are gone.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Ten bodies had been found between May and November 1984 in Florida. A 17-year-old female named Lisa McVey was abducted but released, leading police to the perpetrator, Bobbie Joe Long.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Rosemary Anderson is found dead on the side of the road, her boyfriend becomes the prime suspect. He eventually confessed to her murder, but so did another man - serial killer, Eric Edgar Cooke.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A 19-year-old woman was found dead in her car, the scene had been staged to make murder appear to be suicide. During their investigation, police discovered the victim was involved in a love quadrangle, giving several people a motive to kill her.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: While investigating an apparent overdose suicide, detectives recover incriminating files from a computer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A nurse experiences a variety of flu-like symptoms. None of her doctors are able to discover the cause, until she visits the gynecologist for a routine check-up.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A 1995 fire razes the mansion of a Kansas doctor, killing two of her three children; disturbing accounts from an ex-husband lead investigators to believe the doctor set the blaze herself.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On a cold December night in 1993, Rose Larner stopped in a convenience store on her way to her boyfriend's house. She was never seen or heard from again. A tiny clue found years later revealed a tragic tale of drugs, romance and revenge.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Two people in Seattle, Washington died after taking an over-the-counter pain reliever. Lab analysis of the pills showed they were tainted with a lethal concentration of cyanide.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When the wife of a well-known dentist is found dead, police struggle to identify a suspect. Some fibers and a study of the weather patterns on the night of the murder break the case open.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The wife of a murdered horse rancher explains that the last time she saw her husband was when he was leaving the house to go jogging. Police become suspicious when forensic evidence eventually leads investigators back to the ranch.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When the body of a young woman is found under an isolated highway ramp, a unique fiber on the victim's clothing leads San Diego police straight to the most unlikely of killers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After 6-year-old Michelle Lee Dorr went missing, her father confessed to murdering her. But investigators soon poked holes in his story, leaving the case unsolved for 14 years.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Just weeks before a witness is to testify against the man accused of sexually assaulting her, she is murdered.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police are dispatched to the scene of a shooting, and discover the victim's husband is one of their own: a homicide detective who says his wife accidentally shot herself in the head.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2002, a small community in upstate New York was devastated when Patty Wlasiuk died in a truck accident. But burdock seed pods found in her hair and on her clothes indicated that this was no accident - it was cold-blooded murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When police in the Great Plains are called about a dead body, they do a background check on the victim.

programme.title: Forensic Files
